ads.author: Kenneth Blackmon
ads.title: STAR WARS SIDESHOW YODA AND CLONE TROOPER PREMIUM EDITION STATUE / CLONES
ads.description: Introducing a superb Premium Star Wars Sideshow Statue of Yoda and Clone Troopers, capturing a dramatic moment from the iconic scene shortly after Order 66 was given by Palpatine. This masterpiece, crafted with exquisite detail and precision, brings to life the intensity and emotion of that pivotal moment. Key Features: Impeccable Craftsmanship: Each figure is meticulously sculpted and painted to perfection, capturing the likeness of Yoda and the Clone Troopers with stunning accuracy. Dynamic Poses: The statue portrays Yoda in a dynamic stance, ready for battle, with Clone Troopers in action, creating a captivating and lifelike scene. High-Quality Materials: Made from premium materials, this statue ensures durability and longevity, making it a prized addition to any Star Wars collection. Detailed Base: The base of the statue is intricately designed to resemble the movie's setting, adding an extra layer of authenticity and depth. Limited Edition: This statue is part of a limited edition series, ensuring its exclusivity and value over time. Don't miss the chance to own this exclusive piece of Star Wars history for $2000 plus shipping and handling. Elevate your collection with this remarkable statue and relive the unforgettable moments from the Star Wars saga. Displayed in cases. Original boxes provided.

ads.author: Furniture Closeouts
ads.title: **Upright Arcade Machine**
ads.description: **Upright Arcade Machine** 2 Player (with lots of simultaneous gameplay) Over 1000 games The Upright Arcade Machine has been a favorite for younger clients or families because two players can play at the same time against each other on multiple games (while the Whiskey Barrel and Cocktail machines have games that go back and forth between player one and two for their turns). A lot of kids aren't used to having to wait their turn to play against someone in video games and this is a great addition to any game room. You can't find machines like this anywhere else. The High Score arcade machines stand out because they are 100% custom-designed and built by a team in Tennessee with years of experience. No prebuilt templates or kits, no stock artwork. The High Score understands that making a machine as easy to operate as possible is essential for rental properties, game rooms, break rooms, and businesses. That's why all High Score machines are all plug-and-play (no complicated start up or shut down) and include easy-access volume control.

ads.title: Police Trainer Classic Arcade Game
ads.description: Classic Police Trainer Arcade Game with police Vector light bar and strobes in great condition. This is an original Police Trainer arcade machine made in 1997. Everything works great including the blue lights which you can turn on and off independently. Police Trainer is a target shooting simulation game that takes 1 or 2 players through a series of levels created to help develop skills such as accuracy, speed, decision making, etc., as you compete for high scores. This game has been a lot of fun for our family and friends. It is all target shooting so there's no blood or actual violence; rated for mature responsible ages and individuals. This arcade machine is big and very heavy. If you need it delivered, text me and we might be able to work it out depending on delivery location. Dimensions: H=80in, D=40in, W=32in Asking $1500 but will entertain REASONABLE offers. If you lowball, I won't respond. If you have something to trade, text me and let me know. eight four three five one four nine nine seven five

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: P.O.W. Prisoners of War, Upright Arcade, SNK 1988
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – P.O.W. Prisoners of War (SNK, 1988) Price: $325 Own a piece of late-80s arcade history! This is an original P.O.W. – Prisoners of War upright cabinet from SNK, released in 1988. Known as a classic scrolling fighter / beat ’em up, players battle their way through enemy compounds after escaping captivity. It’s a gritty, action-packed title that helped define the genre and remains a nostalgic favorite for collectors. Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs a lot of love. - Boards need work and likely new caps. - Monitor powers on but the image quality is poor. - Lights come on. - No sound currently. - This is a project cabinet — ideal for restoration enthusiasts. Highlights: - Rare SNK beat ’em up from the golden age of arcades. - Upright cabinet with authentic late-80s design. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a retro collection. Important: - Sold as-is. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: Pole Position, Upright Arcade, Atari/Namco 1982
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – Pole Position (Atari/Namco, November 1982) Price: $400 Step into racing history with this original Pole Position upright cabinet — one of the most iconic arcade racers ever made. Released in 1982 by Atari/Namco, Pole Position was a groundbreaking driving simulator that set the standard for racing games. It’s widely recognized as one of the Top 50 most historically important video games of all time. Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs some love. - Boards need work and likely new caps. - Monitor does not power on. - Lights come on. - No sound currently. - This is a project cabinet — perfect for restoration enthusiasts or collectors. Highlights: - Classic upright racer with simulator-style gameplay. - Rare piece of arcade history from the golden age. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a collection. Important: - Price is negotiable, but only slightly. - Sold as-is. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

A great classic that is priced less than many other arcade games I need to sell around 22k of items to cover upcoming business license and local City real estate taxes. I also have those nagging medical bills as I owe UT for life saving surgery but so happy I could be seen so quickly. Best money ever spent. Anyway I will be listening several different items and once I can get to the total I can stop. A bulk buyer like Scott Wright or Coin Op Warehouse or anyone is very welcome. I can hold for 6 weeks but need payment up front. I have heen in business since 1987 and so far never cheated anyone nor will I. I am very real honest and this is not s scam. More pics available. My cell is [hidden information]. PayPal, wire cash all ok. Located in Norton Va 24273 Thanks for looking. Buddy

ads.title: Classic 1981 Nintendo Donkey Kong Original Arcade Machine in Dedicated Cabinet TKG4-UP-US Blue
ads.description: Up for sale is an authentic piece of gaming history: an all original 1981 Nintendo Donkey Kong upright arcade machine. This is a dedicated cabinet featuring the iconic "Nintendo Blue" finish and original artwork. The machine has been recently brought back to life after years of storage. It is currently in fully playable condition and only needs a final dial-in of the H-hold coil on the monitor in order to be perfect, which I do not know how to do. All original parts with zero modifications or customizations other than bypassing the coin mechanism to allow free play (this can be reversed easily). Pickup in Concord, NC or I can arrange delivery for an extra fee after payment has been made. Cash, Cashapp, Paypal, Money Order or Cashier Check. I will also take trades on gold and silver.

ads.title: 1996 Sega Wave Runner Arcade Machine
ads.description: *If it's up, it's available. Local pickup near Boiling Springs SC. Electronic payments (Cash app, venmo, zelle etc) are not accepted. Cash only please* I had planned on restoring this arcade machine but life got in the way. It powers on and works to an extent but it needs TLC to get it fully functional. The picture convergence needs help. The jet ski needs an air compressor to make the ride functional which is missing so I never could test it. There's also a piece of aluminum trim on the left side of the jet ski that was missing when I got it. The Sega Model 2 inside has been replaced so the old one is included. You will need to bring a trailer with a ramp to load it. Both pieces are on wheels so it's relatively easy to move but very heavy.

ads.author: Richard Redd
ads.title: (RARE) Gottlieb Nudge-It Upright Arcade / Pinball Game, Circa 1990
ads.description: This is an original Nudge-It upright arcade pinball-style game produced by Gottlieb (Premier Technology) around 1990. Nudge-It Is a distinctive and unconventional entry ir Gottlied's late-era catalog, designed without traditional flippers. Instead, gameplay relies on a spring-loaded plunger and controlled cabinet nudging to guide the ball into illuminated scoring lanes, creating a hybric experience between pinball and skill-based arcade play. From a historical perspective, Nudge-It represents one of Gottlieb's experimental designs during the final years of large-scale American pinball manufacturing. Unlike standard pinball machines, the absence of flippers was intentional, encouraging physical interaction and timing rather than reflex-based flipper control. This design makes Nudge-It stand out among collectors as a novelty machine with strong visual appeal and an approachable learning curve for casual players. Production & Rarity: Exact production figures for Nudge-It were not formally published, but collector consensus and industry references generally estimate production at approximately 2,000 units or fewer, making it a relatively low-production title compared to mainstream pinball machines of the era. As a result, surviving examples-particularly complete, powered-on machines-are considered uncommon on today's secondary market.This electrical standing arcade game by Gottlieb stands 22" X 37" X 57" high at the back. The spring-attached knob releases the silver chrome ball perfectly. No flappers were designed for this arcade game. The idea was to "nudge" the ball into the lit lane. This electrical standing arcade game measures approximately 22 inches wide by 37 inches deep and 57 inches high at the back. The spring-attached plunger releases the chrome ball smoothly, and the illuminated playfield elements activate as designed. No flippers were manufactured for this game, as nudging the cabinet is the intended method of ball control. The cabinet, backglass, and playfield artwork display expected cosmetic wear consistent with age and commercial use.
